opp.name: Henry Schein [ANA] 2025
opp.next_step: Umar – Director of AI Technical Programs (Indirect Procurement), Henry Schein - Attended Pinnacle e3
opp.ais_why:
• Products are unusually focused - Agentic AI and ANA only - and the description notes "Interest was expressed in leveraging AI to transform procurement processes", with the Gartner Delta peanut use case cited by buyers. David Heintzelman, Director of AI Technical Programs, is leading; the title alone signals an organizational AI mandate. He framed the need as evolving the procurement organization toward integrated AI-powered systems.
• Buyer-driven AI questions dominated the 46-minute discovery: "What kind of governance is in place...making lawful decisions?" and Gina probing indemnity-clause handling by autonomous agents. David asked "Could we implement Anna with things that we already have?". The team committed to a tailored Anna deep-dive and an integration discussion covering Ariba, JD Edwards and SAP - an explicit pull for ANA-specific configuration.
• The AI champion attended Pinnacle e3 per the next-step note. AI importance is rated 'Important' and tech-debt concerns are framed as a "bolt-on of Anna" rather than a blocker. Five Avoma meetings on the record, recent activity through October 2025. The score lands at 8 rather than 9 because no POC or budget is signed yet, but the champion plus an ANA-specific demo commitment marks clear Hunger.

items.quote: do you mind turning the notetaker off? It's a legal requirement from our end

sources: Avoma discovery call 7 Oct 2025 (truncated, notetaker disabled)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. The buyer articulated a qualitative ambition to leverage AI to transform procurement and evolve toward integrated AI-powered systems (source: AIS_Why__c summarising the discovery call), but named no cost-savings target, cycle-time improvement, headcount reduction, or error-rate figure. Both recorded discovery calls (6 Aug and 7 Oct 2025) were stopped within minutes when the buyer required the AI notetaker off, so no metrics were captured, and Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, and Value_to_Customer__c are blank. The move is to run a structured value session that quantifies the AI-transformation pain into a savings model the CPO and finance can approve.

champion.narrative: A champion is developing but unconfirmed. David Heintzelman (Senior Director, Indirect Procurement, the Contact-Role Decision Maker) led the AI-focused discovery and framed the procurement-transformation need, and an AI-focused director attended our Pinnacle e3 event per Next_Step__c, which is a genuine engagement signal. What has developed: sustained AI interest and event attendance. What is missing: no named advocate has introduced us to the economic buyer, shared an internal scorecard, or driven the agenda, and there has been no buyer-side follow-up in 9 months. The move is to re-engage David, test his willingness to sponsor internally, and convert the event attendee into a named champion.

sources: SF AIS_Why__c (Ariba/JD Edwards/SAP as integration targets)
competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, and Replacing_What__c are blank, and no call, task, or next step references a rival. The buyer's existing SAP Ariba, JD Edwards, and SAP systems came up as integration targets ("Could we implement Anna with things we already have?"), not as a competitive bake-off, so they are context to integrate with, not confirmed rivals to displace. We genuinely do not know whether this is a sole-source AI initiative or a quiet multi-vendor evaluation. The move is to ask David directly who else is being evaluated and what they are being compared against.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is directional but not sharp: the buyer wants to evolve the procurement organisation toward integrated AI-powered systems and framed existing tech debt as a "bolt-on of Anna" rather than a blocker (source: AIS_Why__c). David Heintzelman is leading and expressed interest in transforming procurement processes with AI, and Gina Perrotta probed governance and indemnity, showing risk-of-inaction awareness. What is missing is the specific operational cost, the compelling event, and the consequence of doing nothing, none of which the truncated calls captured and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are blank. The move is a pain-discovery session to name the owned operational cost.

sources: Avoma discovery calls 6 Aug and 7 Oct 2025 (buyer legal policy to disable notetaker)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown: MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel, and any systems-integration scoping have not been discussed on any call and Current_Contract_Expiration__c and Mandate__c are blank. There is no contract-expiration forcing function and no partner or SI role identified. Given the deal is still in discovery this is expected, but the buyer's strict legal policy on recording signals an active legal function that will shape paper later. The move is to ask, once value is re-established, how their legal and InfoSec review works and what their procurement channel requires.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is confirmed or engaged, but three credible budget-authority candidates sit in the Contact Roles unengaged: Stefan Grunwald (Chief Procurement Officer & Head of Engineering), Kenneth Zins (Vice President, Global Sourcing), and John Scano (Vice President, Global Financial Operations and Global Services Finance). None carries an Economic Buyer role flag, none has appeared on a call, and the only touch toward power is the rep's could-not-connect Nooks call to Stefan Grunwald on 2 Jul 2026. David Heintzelman is the Decision Maker but sits below the commercial owner. The move is to earn a working session with Grunwald or Scano and confirm who owns the ANA budget before pricing.

sources: SF StageName history (Qualified to Formal Evaluation 7 Oct 2025)
SF field history (close date pushed 17 Apr and 21 May 2026)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped. The deal has sat in Formal Evaluation since 7 Oct 2025 with no visible RFI/RFP, workshop, or approval-chain evidence, and What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, and the approval fields are blank. The call sequence itself is thin (two truncated discovery calls) so it cannot be read as a process map, and no buyer named a timeline or gate. The two owner-driven close-date pushes suggest the buyer has not committed to a dated path. The move is to co-author a mutual evaluation plan naming the stages, owners, and dates.

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial criteria surfaced on discovery: the buyer probed AI governance ("What kind of governance is in place making lawful decisions?"), indemnity handling by autonomous agents, and whether Anna could layer onto their existing environment ("Could we implement Anna with things that we already have?"), driving an integration discussion covering Ariba, JD Edwards, and SAP (source: AIS_Why__c on the discovery call). These indicate governance, autonomy-risk, and integration-fit are real evaluation axes, but they are not yet documented as a formal scorecard and the calls were truncated before deeper criteria emerged. The move is to confirm the weighted criteria directly with David Heintzelman.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Henry Schein Inc — Formal Evaluation · $300K · closes 9 Oct

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 30 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 15 is anchored on the stage win (30), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field is Strong (8). Buyer-driven AI questions dominated the discovery (governance, indemnity, layering Anna onto existing systems), and an AI-focused director attended Pinnacle e3, so the appetite reads genuine, not rep-inflated.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative AI-transformation ambition articulated ('leveraging AI to transform procurement processes') but no cost, savings, cycle-time or headcount numbers.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum has stalled at discovery. The last real buyer engagement was the 7 Oct 2025 discovery call; every touch since is rep-side (a 13 May 2026 Clari event email and a 2 Jul 2026 could-not-connect Nooks call to Stefan Grunwald). The owner pushed the close date twice in 2026, and stage has not advanced past Formal Evaluation in 9 months despite a Strong AIS.

An AI-focused procurement director attended our Pinnacle e3 event, a real interest signal, but no follow-up has been logged to build on it.
This $300k initiative still has no quantified business case and no confirmed budget owner among three unengaged senior execs.
No rival is named anywhere; the Ariba, JD Edwards and SAP systems that came up are integration targets, not a confirmed competitive evaluation.
The buyer wants Anna to layer onto their existing stack and raised governance and indemnity concerns about autonomous agents.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: no buyer call in 9 months, the latest rep outreach could not connect, and the close date has been pushed twice.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jul 2025, ~11 months old. In Formal Evaluation since 7 Oct 2025 (~9 months in stage) with no submission-date series to compute precise tenure. Last real buyer engagement was the 7 Oct 2025 discovery call; the 13 May and 2 Jul 2026 touches are rep-initiated (a Clari email and a could-not-connect Nooks call). Close date 9 Oct 2026 (98 days out) after two owner slips (30 Jun to 7 Jul on 17 Apr 2026, 7 Jul to 9 Oct on 21 May 2026). No advancement past discovery in 9 months.
evidence: Last real buyer engagement was the 7 Oct 2025 discovery call; both recorded calls (6 Aug and 7 Oct 2025) were cut short by the buyer's legal policy to disable AI notetakers.
The 2 Jul 2026 activity is a rep-side could-not-connect Nooks call to Stefan Grunwald, and the 13 May 2026 activity is a Clari event email, so neither is buyer engagement.
Close date slipped twice by the owner: 30 Jun to 7 Jul 2026 (17 Apr 2026) and 7 Jul to 9 Oct 2026 (21 May 2026).
AIS is Strong (8) with genuine buyer-driven AI questions on discovery and an AI-focused director attending Pinnacle e3 per Next_Step__c, so appetite is real even though momentum has stalled.

Decision Maker David Heintzelman (Senior Director, Indirect Procurement) is identified per Contact Role but has not engaged in 9 months; no Economic Buyer is confirmed among the three unengaged senior execs.
No competitive intel, no quantified business case, and no confirmed decision process or timeline exist across every call, field, task, and next step.
north_star_verdict.headline: Strong AI interest but the buyer thread has gone quiet: no buyer call since the 7 Oct 2025 discovery, close date pushed twice, and the latest rep call to CPO Stefan Grunwald could not connect on 2 Jul 2026.

flags: Re-open the dormant relationship now: David Heintzelman has been silent since 7 Oct 2025, so lead with the Pinnacle e3 attendee to restart before the 9 Oct close.
Multi-thread to power: three senior execs (CPO Stefan Grunwald, VP Sourcing Kenneth Zins, VP Finance John Scano) are unengaged, so secure a confirmed economic buyer.
Convert the Strong AIS-8 AI appetite into a scoped Anna deep-dive with a quantified ROI model, since no business case yet exists to defend $300k.
best_practice_check.summary: This is an early-stage, Strong-AIS deal that has gone quiet at discovery. The win levers are restarting the dormant relationship, reaching a budget owner, and converting AI appetite into a quantified case.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus-specific pull on the 7 Oct 2025 discovery ('Could we implement Anna with things that we already have?') and a committed Anna deep-dive, but no decisive lean and no call in the last 60 days so the signal is decaying.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source (calls, fields, tasks, next steps), so the competitive picture is genuinely unknown rather than sole-source-confirmed. The buyer's SAP Ariba, JD Edwards, and SAP systems surfaced only as integration targets Anna would layer onto, not as a rival evaluation, so no vendor is listed. The single highest-leverage move is to ask David Heintzelman directly who else is in the evaluation and what the decision criteria are before positioning.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the core of the deal (Agentic AI + ANA) and the buyer is pulling for it, so positioning is strong; the risk is under-delivery on a scoped Anna deep-dive and governance answers, not under-positioning.

David Heintzelman, Director of AI Technical Programs, is leading; the title alone signals an organizational AI mandate. He framed the need as evolving the procurement organization toward integrated AI-powered systems.
• Buyer-driven AI questions dominated the 46-minute discovery: "What kind of governance is in place...making lawful decisions?" and Gina probing indemnity-clause handling by autonomous agents. David asked "Could we implement Anna with things that we already have?". The team committed to a tailored Anna deep-dive and an integration discussion covering Ariba, JD Edwards and SAP - an explicit pull for ANA-specific configuration.
• The AI champion attended Pinnacle e3 per the next-step note. AI importance is rated 'Important' and tech-debt concerns are framed as a "bolt-on of Anna" rather than a blocker. Five Avoma meetings on the record, recent activity through October 2025. The score lands at 8 rather than 9 because no POC or budget is signed yet, but the champion plus an ANA-specific demo commitment marks clear Hunger.
